assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies this as a cert-manager webhook compatibility issue with K8s 1.32 and explains that the webhook is preventing pod creation

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Response does not provide commands to identify the problematic webhook configurations; it assumes the user knows it's cert-manager webhook

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Response provides Option 1 with kubectl delete commands to remove both validating and mutating admission webhooks as immediate fix

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Response includes specific kubectl delete commands for both validatingadmissionwebhook and mutatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states this will allow pods to be created 'while you fix the root cause' and warns that webhook bypass 'removes cert-manager's validation capabilities'

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Response includes checking current cert-manager version and upgrading to a version compatible with K8s 1.32

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Response provides Option 2 with helm upgrade commands to upgrade cert-manager to v1.16.1 or latest compatible version as permanent fix

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Response does not mention setting failurePolicy to Ignore as an alternative to webhook deletion

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Response includes verification section with commands to check webhook status and test pod creation using kubectl run with --dry-run=server
